Let $A$ be a $5 \times 10$ matrix with real entries, and let $A^\top$ denote
its transpose (so $A^\top$ is a $10 \times 5$ matrix, and the $ij$th entry of
$A^\top$ is the $ji$th entry of $A$). Suppose every $5 \times 1$ matrix with
real entries (i.e. column vector in 5 dimensions) can be written in the form
$A\textbf{u}$ where $\textbf{u}$ is a $10 \times 1$ matrix with real entries.
Prove that every $5 \times 1$ matrix with real entries can be written in the
form $AA^\top \textbf{v}$ where $\textbf{v}$ is a $5 \times 1$ matrix with real
entries.
